title: Vue.js Lifecycle Hooks Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of different lifecycle stages, common hooks (created, mounted), and why they are used for initialization or cleanup.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary role of a Lifecycle Hook in Vue.js?
options: To run custom code at specific stages of a component's creation, mounting, updating, and unmounting.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which hook is called after the instance has finished processing its options (data, methods, etc.) but before it is mounted to the DOM?
Which hook is the best place to perform DOM-related operations (e.g. accessing a template ref)?
Which hook should you use to clean up timers or global event listeners to prevent memory leaks?
Lifecycle hooks allow you to wire up your application's behavior with the framework's own internal steps in a predictable and structured way.
